health_good_msg: Your connection looks healthy. All values are within normal range.
health_tolerated_msg: Some values show a tolerated deviation from the ideal range. Your ISP considers these acceptable and no action is needed.
health_marginal_msg: Some values are outside the acceptable range. This may not affect your internet experience yet, but should be resolved within a month.
health_poor_msg: One or more values are critically out of range. You may experience connection issues such as slow speeds, dropouts, or high latency.
health_critical_msg: One or more values are critically out of range. You may experience connection issues such as slow speeds, dropouts, or high latency.
issue_ds_power_critical_desc: Signal level is outside the critical range. This usually indicates a cabling or amplifier problem. Contact your ISP if the issue persists.
issue_ds_power_marginal_desc: Signal level is in the marginal range. Should be resolved within a month if it persists.
issue_ds_power_tolerated: Downstream power slightly out of spec
issue_ds_power_tolerated_desc: Signal level shows a tolerated deviation from the ideal range. Your ISP considers this acceptable.
issue_us_power_critical_low_desc: Transmit power is below the critical minimum. The signal from your modem may not be reaching the provider reliably. Check cable connections or request a signal check from your ISP.
issue_us_power_critical_high_desc: Transmit power exceeds the critical maximum. Your modem is working too hard to reach the provider. Check cable connections or request a signal check from your ISP.
issue_us_power_marginal_low_desc: Transmit power is in the marginal low range. Should be investigated if it persists over time.
issue_us_power_marginal_high_desc: Transmit power is in the marginal high range. Should be investigated if it persists over time.
issue_us_power_tolerated_low_desc: Transmit power is slightly below the ideal range. Your ISP considers this a tolerated deviation.
issue_us_power_tolerated_high_desc: Transmit power is slightly above the ideal range. Your ISP considers this a tolerated deviation.
issue_snr_critical_desc: SNR is critically low, causing packet loss and connection instability. Common causes: damaged coax cable, loose connectors, or interference from splitters.
issue_snr_marginal_desc: SNR is in the marginal range. Connection works but should be investigated if it persists.
issue_snr_tolerated_desc: SNR is slightly below the ideal range. Your ISP considers this a tolerated deviation.
issue_us_modulation_marginal_desc: One or more upstream channels have stepped down to a lower modulation (e.g. 16QAM instead of 64QAM). This indicates elevated noise on the return path. Monitor over time and contact your ISP if it persists.
issue_us_modulation_critical_desc: One or more upstream channels are running at a very low modulation (e.g. QPSK or 8QAM). The modem is struggling to maintain a stable upstream. Check cable connections and contact your ISP.
issue_ds_power_warn: Downstream power slightly out of spec
issue_ds_power_warn_desc: Signal level is approaching the -8 to +20 dBmV limits. Not critical yet, but worth monitoring over time.
issue_us_power_warn_low_desc: Transmit power is below the 41 dBmV ideal minimum. May indicate excessive signal attenuation. Monitor this value over time.
issue_us_power_warn_high_desc: Transmit power exceeds the 47 dBmV ideal maximum. May indicate signal loss on the line. Monitor this value over time.
issue_snr_warn_desc: SNR below 33 dB (ideal: above 33 dB). Connection works but has reduced margin for error. Consider checking cable quality.
issue_us_modulation_warn_desc: One or more upstream channels have stepped down to a lower modulation (e.g. 16QAM instead of 64QAM). This indicates elevated noise on the return path. Monitor over time and contact your ISP if it persists.
issue_uncorr_errors_high_desc: Over 10,000 uncorrectable errors detected. These cannot be recovered and cause data loss. If this number grows rapidly, contact your ISP.
issue_uncorr_errors_critical_desc: The uncorrectable error rate has exceeded the critical threshold. This causes packet loss and connection drops. Check your coax cabling and contact your ISP.
no_docsis_msg: DOCSIS channel data is not available in this mode. If you connect via fiber, DSL, or satellite, the metrics below show your connection quality.
no_data: No data available for this period.
modem_user_hint: Tip: Create a dedicated user for DOCSight instead of using your main admin account.
snapshot_hint: Reference time for daily comparisons and trends
export_hint: Copy this text and paste it into your favorite LLM (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, ...) for connection insights.
export_no_data: No data available yet. Wait for the first poll.
login_hint: Enter the admin password to access DOCSight.
login_rate_limited: Too many attempts. Please try again later.
bqm_url_hint: Paste your ThinkBroadband BQM share link (.png)
bqm_no_data: No BQM graph for this date.
card_no_docsis31: No DOCSIS 3.1 upstream channel found. May depend on your plan or equipment.
card_errors_note: Uncorrectable errors cannot be recovered. High growing numbers indicate a cable issue.
tip_ds_power: Signal Level measures how strong the signal from your provider arrives. Ideal is close to 0 dBmV. Too high or too low means a cable or amplifier issue — like a TV picture that's too bright or too dark.
tip_snr: Signal Clarity (SNR) is like radio reception quality. High values = crystal-clear signal. Low values = static and interference, which causes buffering and dropouts.
tip_transmit_power: This is how loudly your modem has to 'shout' to reach your provider. If it's too high, there's probably a cable problem — similar to shouting into a bad phone connection.
tip_uncorrectable: These are data packets that were damaged during transmission and could NOT be repaired. They cause buffering, lag in games, or broken video calls.
tip_correctable: These packets arrived damaged but were automatically repaired. A few are normal, but high numbers are an early warning sign of cable issues.
tip_download: Your actual download speed compared to what you're paying for. Below 80% of your booked speed may indicate a problem.
tip_upload: Your actual upload speed compared to what you're paying for. Important for video calls, cloud backups, and streaming.
bqm_setup_intro: BQM by ThinkBroadband monitors your connection quality by pinging your public IP from external servers. DOCSight can display BQM graphs alongside your modem data for a complete picture.
bqm_setup_benefit_1: Independent proof of connection issues (not just modem-side)
bqm_setup_benefit_2: Tracks latency, packet loss, and throughput 24/7
bqm_setup_benefit_3: Strengthens ISP complaints with third-party evidence
bqm_setup_step_1: Set up DynDNS on your router (e.g. MyFRITZ! on Fritz!Box) so BQM can find your IP
bqm_setup_step_2: Enable WAN ping/ICMP on your router so it responds to external pings (Fritz!Box: Internet → Permit Access → tick "Respond to ping")
bqm_setup_step_3: Go to thinkbroadband.com/broadband/monitoring, create a free account, and register your DynDNS address
bqm_setup_step_4: Once monitoring is active, copy your Share URL (ends in .png) and paste it in DOCSight Settings → BQM Share URL
speedtest_url_hint: Base URL of your Speedtest Tracker instance
speedtest_token_hint: Bearer token from Speedtest Tracker settings
booked_download_hint: Contracted download speed in Mbps for health calculation
booked_upload_hint: Contracted upload speed in Mbps for health calculation
speedtest_setup_intro: Speedtest Tracker is a self-hosted app that runs periodic Ookla speed tests. DOCSight can display your speed test results alongside modem signal data for a complete picture of your connection.
speedtest_setup_benefit_1: Track actual download/upload speeds over time
speedtest_setup_benefit_2: Correlate speed drops with DOCSIS signal issues
speedtest_setup_benefit_3: Independent proof of speed issues for ISP complaints
speedtest_setup_step_2: In Speedtest Tracker, go to Settings → API and create an API token
speedtest_setup_step_3: Paste your Speedtest Tracker URL and API token in DOCSight Settings
event_no_events: No events detected yet. Events are generated automatically when signal conditions change.
signal_no_snapshot: No signal snapshot found within 2 hours of this speedtest.
no_channel_data: No data available for this channel.
modem_test_required: Please test your modem connection first.
review_desc: Review your settings before completing setup.
correlation_desc: Correlate modem signal data, speedtest results, and detected events on a unified timeline to identify patterns and build evidence for ISP complaints.
correlation_no_data: No correlation data available for this period.
vodafone_no_error_counters: Error counters are not available for all Vodafone Station models.
gaming_quality_hint: Show a gaming quality badge in the dashboard hero card based on latency, jitter, and signal health.
gaming_no_speedtest_detail: Connect Speedtest Tracker for full scoring (latency, jitter, packet loss).
gaming_tooltip_c: Playable, but may have occasional issues
gaming_no_data: No data available yet. Waiting for first modem poll.
gaming_comp_latency_detail: Round-trip time to server. <20ms excellent, >120ms critical for gaming.
gaming_comp_jitter_detail: Variation in latency. Low jitter means consistent response times.
gaming_comp_packet_loss_detail: Percentage of lost packets. Even 1% causes noticeable lag spikes.
gaming_comp_docsis_health_detail: Overall cable signal health based on power levels, SNR, and error rates.
gaming_comp_snr_headroom_detail: Signal-to-noise margin above minimum threshold. More headroom means more stability.
gaming_genre_fps_a: Perfect. Competitive play at the highest level.
gaming_genre_fps_b: Great for ranked play. Minimal disadvantage.
gaming_genre_fps_f: Not playable. Too much lag and packet loss.
gaming_genre_moba_a: Perfect. No input delay or ability lag.
gaming_genre_mmo_b: Great. All content playable without issues.
gaming_genre_mmo_c: Good for PvE. PvP may feel sluggish.
gaming_genre_mmo_d: PvE OK, but expect occasional lag spikes.
gaming_genre_strategy_c: Fully playable. No impact on gameplay.
gaming_genre_strategy_f: Turn-based OK, but connection drops possible.
bnetz_upload_hint: PDF from the official BNetzA broadband measurement tool
bnetz_parse_error: Could not read PDF. Is it an official BNetzA measurement protocol?
bnetz_watcher_hint: Automatically imports BNetzA measurement PDFs and CSVs from a watched directory
bnetz_watch_dir_hint: Path where measurement files are placed (e.g., by a sidecar container)
bnetz_upload_csv_hint: Upload PDF or CSV measurement files
bnetz_docker_hint: See the wiki for a Docker Compose example with the official BNetzA Desktop App sidecar
demo_migrate_desc: You are running in demo mode with sample data. Switch to live mode to start monitoring your real modem. Your own journal entries and incidents will be preserved.
demo_migrate_confirm: This will delete all demo data and switch to live mode. Your own entries will be kept. Continue?
demo_migrate_success: Successfully switched to live mode. Demo data has been removed.
bqm_no_data_range: No BQM data in this range
bqm_import_drop: Drag & drop images here or click to select
bqm_collect_time_hint: Time of day to fetch the BQM graph (container timezone)
setup_start_desc: How would you like to get started?
restore_desc: Upload a DOCSight backup file to restore your data
timezone_hint: Use an IANA timezone (e.g. Europe/Berlin) to ensure correct daylight saving time handling.
timezone_posix_warning: Your container uses a POSIX timezone that does not support daylight saving time. Select an IANA timezone (e.g. Europe/Berlin) to fix this.
api_tokens_desc: Create tokens for programmatic API access (e.g. Home Assistant, scripts)
api_token_created: Token created. Copy it now — it won't be shown again.
api_token_copy_warning: This token will only be shown once. Store it securely.
api_token_revoke_confirm: Revoke token "{name}"? Any system using it will lose access.
modules_restart_banner: Module changes require a restart to take effect.
notify_webhook_url_hint: HTTP POST endpoint (ntfy, Discord, Gotify, custom)
notify_min_severity_hint: Only send events at or above this severity
notify_cooldown_hint: Min. seconds between same event type (0 = never send)
hysteresis_desc: Prevent health status flapping near threshold boundaries
hysteresis_count_hint: Number of consecutive polls before confirming a health state change (0 = instant, recommended: 3)
notify_per_event_desc: Disable events or set individual cooldown timers
notify_disabled_hint: Disabled events will never be sent. Cooldown empty = use default.
notify_test_desc: Send a test event to verify your webhook configuration
support_subtitle: Help keep this project alive and growing
support_description: DOCSight began as a personal fight with unstable cable internet and support replies that never matched reality. I wanted a tool that turns frustration into clear technical proof people can actually use. If you want to support that mission, thank you. It really helps.
setup_fresh_desc: Configure a new DOCSight instance from scratch
setup_restore_desc: Upload a previous backup to restore your data
review_settings_desc: Review your configuration and complete setup
poll_interval_desc: How often to fetch DOCSIS data
glossary_snr: Signal-to-Noise Ratio in decibels. Higher is better. Good: above 33 dB (SC-QAM) or 40 dB (OFDM). Below 30 dB causes packet loss, buffering, and slow speeds.
glossary_ds_power: Downstream signal strength in dBmV. Good range: -4 to +13 dBmV. Values outside -8 to +20 dBmV are critical. Too low means weak signal, too high means overdriven amplifier.
glossary_us_power: Upstream transmit power in dBmV. Good range: 41 to 47 dBmV. Values outside 35 to 53 dBmV are critical. High values mean the modem is working harder to reach the ISP.
glossary_errors: Correctable errors (FEC): damaged packets that were automatically repaired. Uncorrectable errors (CRC): lost packets causing buffering and dropouts. A ratio above 1% uncorrectable is critical.
glossary_scqam: SC-QAM (Single-Carrier QAM): traditional DOCSIS 3.0 technology using narrow 6-8 MHz channels for individual data streams.
glossary_ofdm: OFDM/OFDMA: DOCSIS 3.1 technology using wide channels with thousands of subcarriers. Enables higher speeds but is more sensitive to interference.
glossary_modulation: Modulation (QAM level): higher levels like 256QAM or 4096QAM carry more data per signal. Lower modulation indicates signal degradation.
glossary_docsis: DOCSIS 3.0 uses SC-QAM channels. DOCSIS 3.1 adds OFDM/OFDMA for higher speeds. Most modems use both simultaneously (mixed mode).
glossary_gaming_index: Gaming Quality Index: rates your connection for real-time gaming based on latency, jitter, and packet loss. A/B = great, C = playable, D/F = problematic.
color_mode_desc: Switch between dark and light mode
dark_mode_hint: Toggle between dark and light theme
installed_themes_desc: Select a theme to apply across the entire app
available_themes_desc: Browse and install themes from the community
data_collection_desc: Configure polling interval, data retention and daily snapshots
seg_subtitle: Cable segment utilization from FRITZ!Box monitoring.
seg_no_data: No segment utilization data collected yet. Enable polling in Settings.
seg_note: Segment utilization values are estimates provided by the FRITZ!Box and may differ from actual CMTS-wide load.
seg_unsupported_driver: This view is only available for FRITZ!Box cable devices.
